name: Colina de la Muntera – circular desde Corbera d'Ebre por el espacio histórico de las Devees
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Corbera D'Ebre traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, historical sites, and natural waterways. The region offers varied terrain, including paths through agricultural areas and routes exploring local historical spaces. Mountain bikers can expect a mix of surfaces and elevations suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Corbera D'Ebre The most popular mountain bike trail is Colina de la Muntera – circular desde Corbera d'Ebre por el espacio histórico de las Devees, a 17.7 miles (28.4 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This route explores historical areas and offers varied terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Waterway – Challenging trail loop from Gandesa, a moderate 24.2 miles (39.0 km) path. This route follows a waterway, providing scenic views and varied surfaces. Local mountain bikers also love the Fontcalda – Vintage Train Car Café loop from Corbera d'Ebre, a 21.8 miles (35.2 km) trail leading through the Fontcalda area, known for its natural springs and historical railway features, often completed in about 3 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Corbera D'Ebre is defined by rolling hills, historical sites, and natural waterways. The network includes options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike routes. The routes in Corbera D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Corbera D'Ebre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Corbera D'Ebre?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Corbera D'Ebre, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 5 easy, 41 moderate, and 16 difficult routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Corbera D'Ebre?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking in Corbera D'Ebre, with milder temperatures and less chance of extreme heat. Summers can be very hot, while winters are typically mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Corbera D'Ebre?
A: Yes, Corbera D'Ebre offers some easier routes suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always clear, routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances, often found among the 5 easy trails, are a good starting point. Always check the route details for suitability.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with historical significance in the area?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946070976/colina-de-la-muntera-circular-desde-corbera-d-ebre-por-el-espacio-historico-de-las-devees" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colina de la Muntera – circular desde Corbera d'Ebre por el espacio histórico de las Devees&lt;/a&gt; route explores historical areas, offering a blend of riding and cultural insight.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Corbera D'Ebre?
A: Many of the mountain bike trails in Corbera D'Ebre are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17512390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gandesa – Gandesa Boundary Cross loop from Gandesa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Corbera D'Ebre?
A: Mountain bike trails in Corbera D'Ebre feature varied terrain, including paths through rolling hills, agricultural areas, and natural waterways. You can expect a mix of surfaces, from gravel tracks to more technical singletrack, suitable for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any natural features or viewpoints to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1044274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda&lt;/a&gt; area, known for its natural springs, is a notable natural feature. You can explore this area via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31499175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontcalda – Vintage Train Car Café loop from Corbera d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Where can I find a café or a place to rest along the mountain bike routes?
A: Some routes pass by convenient stops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19830220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus) – Fontcalda loop from Corbera d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/634991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular spot for a break.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Corbera D'Ebre?
A: The mountain bike trails in Corbera D'Ebre are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails that follow waterways or offer scenic views of rivers?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate natural waterways. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23760626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterway – Challenging trail loop from Gandesa&lt;/a&gt; is a good example, following a waterway and providing scenic views along its course.

Q: Are there options for advanced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Corbera D'Ebre offers 16 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. These trails typically feature more significant elevation gains and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19830220" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bot Station Bar (Railbus) – Fontcalda loop from Corbera d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt; is one such challenging route, covering a longer distance with substantial climbing.
tips: Place built on an old trench from the battle of the Ebro in memory of the combatants who fought in the battle of the Ebro.
Located on top of a hill, the streets and ruined houses of the Old Town of Corbera d'Ebre are testimony to the consequences of war and violence during the Battle of the Ebro.
